understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting all goal tiles marked with `+` occupied by selectable pieces at the same time (i.e., every `+` must have a piece on it simultaneously for WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece. Arrow actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempt to move the selected piece one grid step; movement can fail when blocked by walls/obstacles. CLICK attempts to move the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate; this sometimes succeeds (e.g., [15,21] -> [9,21]) and sometimes does nothing when the target is blocked or otherwise invalid (e.g., clicks at [33,15], [37,19], [35,17], [38,20]). I did not observe any successful pushing interactions in these turns; failed moves/clicks appear to be due to collision/blocked destinations.

orient_history: Movement is impossible from current enclosure, so progress likely requires CLICK-based interaction (e.g., selecting/activating the goal or triggering a teleport/swap mechanic by clicking on a goal tile/object).
Hypothesis: CLICK selects a different piece; movement applies only to the selected piece. Current piece cannot move, so we must switch to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: MOVE actions shift the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but collision with walls blocks. CLICK may create/select a selectable at the clicked location if that location is a valid empty/goal tile; the earlier goal click may have missed the valid pixels for the goal tile. To make progress despite being boxed in, try CLICK directly within the goal object's defined bounds to attempt to place/create a selectable onto the goal.

CLICK may be able to select/manipulate non-selectable collidable objects (e.g., walls) to open a path, since neither selectable can MOVE. I will test whether a wall tile can be selected/affected by clicking it.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works when adjacent cells are free (currently fully blocked). CLICK attempts to relocate the currently selected piece to the exact clicked coordinate (often grid-aligned), but the move is rejected if the destination is invalid or blocked; aiming at the goal’s exact position may be required to register/allow placement.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): MOVE_* actions may change which selectable is controlled (or attempt a grid-step move) even when CLICK-to-teleport is blocked; selection might need to switch to the other piece to make progress toward the goal.

Clicks can either (a) select a different selectable when clicking on it, or (b) attempt to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate if reachable/allowed. Since the current selected piece is fully blocked for MOVE, progress likely requires switching to the other selectable and moving it instead.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected (if the click lands within its 3x3 area). MOVE_* then moves the selected piece by one grid step (3 px) if not blocked. We need to select the other piece to explore a different mobility region toward the goal cluster.

Working hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts by 1 cell (3 px) but can be blocked by walls; CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate (or nearest valid spot) if the destination/path is valid. Earlier goal-clicks may have failed due to clicking non-grid-aligned or blocked cells. Try a grid-aligned point inside the goal region.

Working hypothesis: CLICK can change which selectable is active and/or swap positions when clicking on the other selectable; long-distance CLICK moves may depend on which piece is selected or on some reachability constraint. Since the current selected piece is fully trapped, switching to the other selectable may enable a different reachable move set.

Arrow-key moves shift the selected piece by one cell (3px) when not blocked. CLICKing on a selectable likely switches control to that piece (prior logs suggest clicks at piece positions cause apparent 'moves' that may actually be selection changes). To make progress, switch to the other selectable and probe its mobility toward the goal area.

New hypothesis: MOVE_* performs a 1-cell (3-pixel) step for the selected piece, and the movement-blocked flags may be unreliable or context-dependent (since we previously moved between [15,21] and [18,21]). Progress likely requires stepping back to [18,21] using MOVE_RIGHT, then exploring further from there.

CLICK likely changes which piece is selected (and/or attempts to move selection to the clicked cell). Since the currently selected piece is boxed in, we should try switching control to the other selectable to regain mobility and attempt reaching the goal from a different position.

CLICK likely either (a) switches selection if you click another selectable, or (b) issues a move/slide command toward the clicked position but stops when blocked by walls. Since arrow moves are fully blocked here, we should try switching to the other selectable to see if it has different mobility or access routes.

CLICK seems to change which selectable is controlled (previous clicks to [9,21] switched control there). To make progress, I should switch to the other selectable and see if it has available movement toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked (grid-snapped) destination if that destination is legal (not colliding). Selecting the other selectable is possible by clicking its location. Goal tiles might be legal destinations; need to click directly on the goal object's pixel position to test.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece under the cursor (switches which piece is the controlled 'player'). MOVE_* then attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls. To make progress, I need to select the other piece and check whether it has any unblocked movement options.

Clicks previously caused the selected piece to jump between the two selectable positions, suggesting CLICK may teleport/swap between selectables rather than move through corridors. MOVE actions appear to be normal grid steps, but the currently selected piece is boxed in; however, MOVE might also change which selectable is active when movement is blocked.

CLICK on the other selectable swaps the selected piece with it (effectively teleporting between the two positions). MOVE_* moves 1 cell (3 pixels) but only if not blocked by walls/out-of-bounds. Since the currently selected piece is boxed in, swapping selection/position may be the only way to regain mobility.

CLICK appears to switch control to the selectable located at the clicked position (rather than moving through walls). MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) when not blocked; different pockets may have different exits.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts step 1 cell (3px) and respects wall collision; CLICK attempts to relocate the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate when that destination is valid/reachable (and has worked previously for [18,21]), while clicks to the goal ([35,17]) failed likely due to blockage/no valid path/line-of-sight. Next I should return to a known valid open square ([18,21]) to search for a click path toward the goal area.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently selected (it only has an effect when clicking on an existing selectable’s position). MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (one cell) but is prevented by collidable walls/pieces.

CLICK may allow teleporting the selected piece to the clicked cell if it is a valid empty/goal tile; the earlier failed goal-click likely used a pixel coordinate not aligned to the cell grid (3 pixels per cell), hitting a wall/invalid spot. To reach the goal region, we should CLICK a grid-aligned pixel coordinate inside a + cell (e.g., cell (11,5) -> pixel [33,15]).

New hypothesis (discarding prior): the level is a tight maze where keyboard moves are the primary way to navigate, and CLICK only repositions/chooses pieces within constrained spaces. Since the current selected piece is immobile, a blocked MOVE might switch selection focus to the other selectable (or otherwise change state) even if position cannot change.
CLICK on a selectable likely changes the active/selected piece (or swaps/moves onto that piece), which may be necessary since the currently selected piece cannot move by MOVE actions.

CLICK appears to be a move-to action (teleport/slide) toward a clicked coordinate when valid; clicking empty/invalid/blocked coordinates causes no movement. MOVE_* is only 1-cell stepping and is currently impossible for the selected piece due to immediate collisions.

Hypothesis: CLICK on an occupied selectable may switch which piece is selected (without needing to move), enabling moving both pieces onto the goal region via subsequent CLICKs. CLICK on empty tiles may teleport the selected piece if the destination is valid/unblocked.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (and may have been interpreted as the selected piece 'moving' when selection switched). If we switch to the other selectable at [15,21], it may have an unblocked move that can free space and allow progress toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate if that destination is valid/reachable per hidden rules (possibly needs to be within the goal region or on a legal cell). MOVE is constrained by collisions, but CLICK can sometimes bypass local blocking if the destination is permitted.

New hypothesis (based only on observed outcomes): CLICK sets a destination and the selected piece moves only if there is a valid unobstructed placement/slide; otherwise it does nothing. MOVE_* attempts grid-step motion but can be blocked by walls or other selectables. Because a selectable is adjacent on the left, a MOVE_LEFT might trigger a different interaction (swap/push/change selection) even when simple movement is reported as blocked.

CLICK attempts can sometimes reposition the selected piece directly to a target pixel coordinate if the engine deems the destination reachable/valid; MOVE actions obey immediate collision blocking. Since only 1 step remains, the only remaining chance is a direct CLICK into the goal area to try to place the selected piece onto the goal and trigger WIN.

Movement occurs in 3-pixel (1 cell) increments; MOVE_* is blocked by adjacent wall tiles. CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked cell but only succeeds if there is a valid path/line-of-action not blocked by walls; clicks near/inside the goal have been blocked from the current compartment. With no steps left, only a selection change (if allowed without consuming steps) could still be useful.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting all goal tiles marked with `+` occupied by selectable pieces at the same time (i.e., every `+` must have a piece on it simultaneously for WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece. Arrow actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempt to move the selected piece one grid step; movement can fail when blocked by walls/obstacles. CLICK attempts to move the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate; this sometimes succeeds (e.g., [15,21] -> [9,21]) and sometimes does nothing when the target is blocked or otherwise invalid (e.g., clicks at [33,15], [37,19], [35,17], [38,20]). I did not observe any successful pushing interactions in these turns; failed moves/clicks appear to be due to collision/blocked destinations.
